articles.title: Nebius Shares Are Climbing Monday: What's Driving The Move?
articles.description: Nebius Group NV (NASDAQ: NBIS ) shares are surging on Monday as traders anticipate the upcoming Nasdaq-100 index reconstitution and what it can mean for forced passive-fund buying ahead of the change taking effect. The move is getting extra lift from a risk-on tape, with the Nasdaq-100 up 2.75% in the regular session. Here’s what investors need to know . Nebius Group stock is showing exceptional strength. Why is NBIS stock surging? What Is Driving Nebius Group’s Nasdaq-100 Inclusion? Nasdaq's quarterly reconstitution adds Nebius to the Nasdaq-100, effective before the market opens June 22, a setup that can pull in incremental demand as index-tracking funds and ETFs position ahead of the rebalance. The same reshuffle adds Astera Labs , CoreWeave , Rocket Lab and Teradyne , while Charter Communications , Cognizant , Insmed , Verisk Analytics and Zscaler exit. Nebius is also getting a halo from the same rebalance chatter that's lifting other additions, as Jim Cramer explicitly called the change a "big deal". With the S&P 500 up 1.63% and the Dow Jones up 1.27% in the regular session, the broader bid for growth is also supporting high-beta names that already have strong momentum. Critical Price Levels To Watch For NBIS Stock Nebius is still in a powerful long-term uptrend: the stock is trading 12.9% above its 20-day SMA ($224.33), 37.2% above its 50-day SMA ($184.61), and 109.4% above its 200-day SMA ($120.96). Those distances matter because they show ... Full story available on Benzinga.com

articles.title: 'That Is Really Nuts,' Says Joe Rogan After Learning Nearly 1 In 10 Credit Card Debts Default. 'One Of The Biggest Problems That Most People Face'
articles.description: Millions of Americans are falling behind on debt payments, and the latest data suggest financial stress is continuing to build. On a recent episode of the “ Joe Rogan Experience ” podcast, personal finance creator Caleb Hammer shared some eye-opening statistics that caught comedian and host Joe Rogan off guard. Hammer told Rogan that Americans currently hold about $1.6 trillion in credit card debt and that roughly 7% of credit card accounts end up in default. “That’s crazy,” Rogan replied. Rogan noticed that the figure is close to 1 in 10 accounts and responded, “That is really nuts.” Don't Miss: Paying too much toward debt? Find out how much you could save with a quick, free check. Need extra retirement cash flow? Homeowners with significant equity are exploring alternatives to traditional HELOCs. Debt Problems Are Growing Across The Board The discussion comes at a time when new data suggest Americans are facing increasing financial pressure . According to a May report by The Kobeissi Letter, serious credit card delinquencies rose to 13.1% during Q1 2026, the highest level since Q4 2010. BREAKING: Credit card serious delinquencies rose +0.4 percentage points in Q1 2026, to 13.1%, the highest since Q4 2010. This is only below the 2010 peak of 13.7%, in the aftermath of the 2008 Financial Crisis. Since Q3 2022, serious credit card delinquencies have surged +5.5... pic.twitter.com/T73WW0goYL — The Kobeissi Letter (@KobeissiLetter) May 19, 2026 The report said that serious credit card delinquencies have climbed 5.5% since Q3 2022, a larger increase than the rise seen during the years surrounding the 2008 financial crisis. Hammer also said that Americans carry more auto loan debt than credit card debt, a statistic that surprised Rogan. “Really?” Rogan asked. The Kobeissi Letter said that auto loan serious delinquencies reached a record 5.6%, while student loan serious delinquencies climbed to 10.3%, the highest level since early 2020. Trending: Explore whether your retirement strategy is optimized for income, taxes, and long-term withdrawals — take the AdviserMatch quiz today. Why Financial Education Matters Hammer’s own story mirrors the financial struggles he now covers on his YouTube show , “Financial Audit.” He told Rogan that he accumulated student ...
